approx_date: 2011-03 (initial disclosure); restated financials filed later in 2011
event_description: Weatherford International Ltd. disclosed in March 2011 that it had identified material weaknesses in its internal control over financial reporting related to its income tax accounting, which required a restatement of prior-period financial statements (multiple fiscal years, roughly 2007–2010). The company announced this would delay filing of its Form 10-K and triggered an Item 4.02 non-reliance notification regarding previously issued financial statements. This accounting issue also drew scrutiny that fed into a broader SEC investigation of the company (which separately also involved FCPA/bribery-related matters resolved around 2013–2014). I recall there being associated leadership/finance-function changes tied to the internal control remediation effort, though I do not have confident, specific recall of a named CFO departure explicitly tied to this restatement (my knowledge on that specific personnel detail is not reliable).
